description: Add a `gbiv cleanup [<color>]` command that detects if a color worktree's feature branch is merged, then checks out the color branch, pulls latest, and removes the associated GBIV.md entry.

userStories.title: Add checkout_branch helper to git_utils
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable `checkout_branch(path, branch)` function in `git_utils.rs` so that the cleanup command can switch branches using the established ProcessCommand pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: `checkout_branch(path: &Path, branch: &str) -> Result<(), String>` added to `src/git_utils.rs`
Runs `git checkout <branch>` in the given directory
Returns an Err with the git stderr output on failure
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add pull helper to git_utils
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable `pull(path)` function in `git_utils.rs` so that the cleanup command can fetch and merge remote changes using the established ProcessCommand pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: `pull(path: &Path) -> Result<(), String>` added to `src/git_utils.rs`
Runs `git pull` in the given directory
Returns an Err with the git stderr output on failure
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add remove_gbiv_features_by_tag to gbiv_md
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a `remove_gbiv_features_by_tag(path, tag)` function in `gbiv_md.rs` so that the cleanup command can remove a color's feature entries from GBIV.md while preserving the rest of the file.
acceptanceCriteria: `remove_gbiv_features_by_tag(path: &Path, tag: &str) -> Result<(), String>` added to `src/gbiv_md.rs`
Removes all entries whose tag matches the given color and writes the file back
When no matching entry exists, the file is unchanged and no error is raised
When multiple entries share the same tag, all are removed
Content after the `---` separator is preserved unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement cleanup_one function
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `cleanup_one(gbiv_root, color) -> Result<(), String>` in `src/commands/cleanup.rs` so that per-color cleanup logic is encapsulated and testable.
acceptanceCriteria: `src/commands/cleanup.rs` created with `cleanup_one` function
Returns Ok with a notice when the worktree is already on the color branch
Returns Err when no remote is configured for the worktree
Returns Err when the feature branch is not merged into remote main
Calls `checkout_branch` then `pull` when branch is merged
Calls `remove_gbiv_features_by_tag` after successful checkout and pull
Returns Err surfacing the git message when checkout fails due to dirty working tree
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement cleanup_command dispatcher
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `cleanup_command(color: Option<&str>) -> Result<(), String>` in `src/commands/cleanup.rs` so that the CLI can invoke cleanup for one or all color worktrees.
acceptanceCriteria: `cleanup_command(color: Option<&str>) -> Result<(), String>` present in `src/commands/cleanup.rs`
When a color is provided, calls `cleanup_one` for that color only and propagates any error
When no color is provided, iterates all ROYGBIV colors, calling `cleanup_one` for each
In all-color mode, a non-fatal error (not merged, missing worktree, already clean) prints a warning and continues to the next color
Returns Err immediately if `find_gbiv_root` fails
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Register cleanup module in commands/mod.rs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need `pub mod cleanup;` added to `src/commands/mod.rs` so that the cleanup module is part of the compiled crate.
acceptanceCriteria: `pub mod cleanup;` present in `src/commands/mod.rs`
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Wire cleanup subcommand into the CLI
userStories.description: As a user, I want `gbiv cleanup [<color>]` available in the CLI so that I can invoke it from the terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: `cleanup` subcommand added to `cli()` in `src/main.rs` via clap
Optional positional `color` argument accepted and validated against the ROYGBIV `COLORS` array
Invalid color value causes clap to exit with a non-zero status and error message
`Some(("cleanup", sub_matches))` arm added in `main()` dispatching to `cleanup_command`
`gbiv cleanup --help` prints usage including the optional color argument
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Unit test remove_gbiv_features_by_tag
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for `remove_gbiv_features_by_tag` so that GBIV.md mutation behavior is verified and protected from regression.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: removes the matching `[color]` entry from GBIV.md
Test: no-op when no entry with that tag exists
Test: removes multiple entries sharing the same tag
Test: content after `---` separator is unchanged after write
All tests pass via `cargo test`

userStories.title: Unit test cleanup_one edge cases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need unit tests for `cleanup_one` key behavioral branches so that merge detection and skip logic are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Test: returns Ok with skip notice when worktree is already on the color branch
Test: returns Err when no remote is configured
Test: returns Err when feature branch is not merged into remote main
All tests pass via `cargo test`

userStories.title: Run full test suite
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm `cargo test` passes with all new and existing tests so that the implementation is complete and non-regressing.
acceptanceCriteria: `cargo test` exits with status 0
No existing tests are broken
All new tests added in US-008 and US-009 pass
